old_claim.note: Bone Tool Making provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The launch-quality anchor is an incised or notched bone artifact, so bone-tool working provides the documented material path without making bone the only possible tally medium.

source_shape.source_locator: Organic-artifact discussion and figure set: reports early Later Stone Age notched/incised bones from Border Cave, including an incised baboon fibula interpreted as a number-keeping device.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes the scoped early tally-like evidence as an incised or notched bone artifact from Border Cave.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the corrected node is anchored to a worked bone object, bone-tool making is a documented material enabler, while wood or other tally media remain outside this first-known anchor.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: bone_tool_making remains an enabling prerequisite, now tied to the source-backed notched-bone anchor.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to wooden tally sticks rather than the Border Cave notched-bone evidence.
A stronger source showed the Border Cave incised bone was not worked as a bone artifact.
The bone_tool_making node were narrowed away from shaping or incising bone artifacts.
short_reason: The launch-quality tally-like evidence is an incised or notched bone artifact.
